meta.name: Heretics 40K
notes: Single source of truth for all game content and rules. The engine (heretics-40k engine HTML) renders this file.

Scope v1.2: Orbit is now a real named location on every planet (tier 'orbit', holds the ships that used to live on planet.orbit); every Sector gains a Space layer (sector.space). Travel Threads, fleets, and orbital actions attach to these. Location types 'orbit' and 'space' added.
Doors are GRANTED BY location type, not stored per location. Conditions take them away. Access is allegiance-gated.
Filename stays heretics-40k-data-v1.json across the v1.x series; meta.version carries the real version. Git carries the history.
Scores / production / resources are specced here but require persistence (Stage 2) to run. Static demo reads them as constants.

v1.8: full Tag Registry migrated from Notion to canon (D.tags: 22 weapon / 11 item / 4 combat / 7 cast-gate / 4 model-state) + per-faction forge_affinities. Engine: GLOSS tooltips for every tag, parseItem recognises the full tag set, the Forge offers the full weapon-tag list gated by faction affinity.

done: more placed NPCs with overview sheets (v1.3)

next.task: Full weapons: Tag Registry + Standard Weapons + Named/Legendary from Tags/Basic Weapons/Named Weapons pages
Full items + abilities from Basic Items / Named & Legendary Items / Abilities pages
Full casts (8 families) from Warp Casting page
Minted planets, populated map, territory ownership per Galaxy & Territory v1

growth.force_xp: Forces earn and rank together; requirement = average of members; joining only at Force's current rank

loadout.model: Free-form universal slots. A model has (base sl + slot_gains_by_rank[class][rank-1]) GENERAL slots plus one separate hard ARMOUR slot. The player assigns each general slot a type (Weapon/Item/Ability/Warp Cast), then equips a matching item. Any slot may take any type — no psyker gate, no reserved legendary slot. Re-typing/equipping is allowed only while the model is NOT in an active thread.
loadout.armour_slot: Every model has one extra hard Armour slot, always present, outside the general budget; holds only cat:ARMOUR. Pre-filled with a class+faction default.

armour.mitigation: Per hit: taken = max(0, dealt - def[element]). Floored at 0, so heavy armour can fully turn a weak hit. Applied per hit (multi-hit resolves each hit separately). The killing element still sets the revival window.
armour.corrosive_note: There is no hardcoded bypass rule. Corrosive-bypass is expressed as data: most armour carries a low or 0 Corrosive defense.

combat.ap_cost: model AP + equipment AP modifier (additive); Effortless = model AP only
combat.desperation_action: When Force AP pool is empty: 1 action per model per post, limited to Movement or Effortless-tagged use

death.body_state_rule: element of the final point of damage decides the body's state; DoT ticks count

succession.heir: AP-holding Named leader, per declared Succession Order

death.field_recovery: victor recovers 50% of their own revivable dead

revival_window.note: Death is a race, not a flag. The killing element sets the WINDOW; the body must reach an Apothecarion within it. Windows are tuned to travel: even the harshest kill leaves a same-sector ride plus one post; softer deaths leave enough that an Apothecary or the right item can turn the tide mid-thread.
revival_window.rule: On death the body enters a revival window measured in posts. It must physically reach a location with an Apothecarion door before the window expires. Travel costs count against the window.
revival_window.minimum_rationale: Harsh-element minimum (3) = same-sector travel (2 posts) + 1. Soft elements add +5 so revival stays a live tactic during and just after a combat thread.

no_revival.rule: A kill dealt by a source carrying a no-revival tag denies the window entirely: window 0, permanent death, no Apothecarion, no cost, no succession-revival. These are the annihilation weapons - use is rare and expensive.

revival_window.expiry: Window expires -> permanent death. No door, no cost, no appeal.
revival_window.commanders: Commanders use the same windows. Succession still fires if the window expires.

against_travel_ladder.cross-sector (3 posts): for harsh kills, already lost; for Physical/Energy, a real chance
against_travel_ladder.cross-segmentum (5 posts): a funeral for all but the freshest Physical/Energy body

revival_window.cost: Apothecarion revival costs Currency, scaled to the model's PC.
revival_window.supersedes: Window lengths (v1.5) supersede the earlier 3/1 values. Field recovery (50%) unchanged for Physical/Energy.

economy.dominance: Commander's total AP; dispatchable to Named models, granting the Leader tag; a Force without AP takes no actions
economy.influence: ownership ceiling: total PC of all controlled models may never exceed Influence, even split across Forces

thread_types.desc: Spawned by movement; arrival procedures resolve here

rules.comms: In-character remote messaging (vox/astropathic) for NPCs/PCs NOT on the same planet/sector. All face-to-face interaction, including Diplomacy, happens in Threads.

naming_rite.eligibility: Rank 5 unnamed model under a Commander
grants: automatic Named delta for its class (see rules.deltas.named)
repeat of the faction Origin Perk choice from Character Creation (choose 2)

perk_effects.psy: Psyker tag - Warp Cast slot opens

chaos.name: CHAOS
chaos.prime_drive: Damnation and ascension — power torn from the warp, the galaxy remade in the Dark Gods' image.
chaos.lens: Reads every event as an omen or an opportunity for the Ruinous Powers; a warp storm is a blessing, order an affront.

imperial.name: IMPERIAL
imperial.prime_drive: The survival of the Imperium of Man — compliance, purity, and the extermination of the alien and the heretic.
imperial.lens: Reads every event through duty and threat; the unknown is presumed hostile, deviation is presumed corruption.

xenos.name: XENOS
xenos.wallets: per-faction currency; Dominance/Influence names TBD per faction
xenos.prime_drive: The ends of an alien people — be it survival, expansion, plunder, or an inhuman design no human fully grasps.
xenos.lens: Reads events by its own species' logic; humanity is one factor among many, rarely the centre.

factions.name: Black Legion
backgrounds: You led a claw when the fortress-world died.
appearances: The Eye seared across the faceplate.

factions.name: Death Guard
backgrounds: The Seventh Bell tolled for you alone.
A plague-hulk's last officer; the crew obeys.
Given a seed to plant among the stars.
appearances: Split plate parted by patient flesh.
Ceramite the color of old sickness.

factions.name: World Eaters
backgrounds: The Nails fell silent once. You hunt that hour.

factions.name: Thousand Sons
backgrounds: Sole survivor of one page too far.
Nine urns that counsel you in dreams.
A relic scattered across the Segmentum.
appearances: Sapphire and gilt, nothing inside but purpose.

factions.name: Emperor's Children
backgrounds: You heard the chord and survived.
Rebuilt by a mad chirurgeon. You approve.
appearances: Pupils blown wide, all felt twice.
A cloak that used to sing.

factions.name: Daemons
backgrounds: Clawed free of a greater shadow.
A ward failed. You remember the binder.
All four thrones. None minds. Yet.
appearances: Flesh deciding its form one moment late.
Brass, rot, silk and fire in one hide.
Too wide, in a wrong place.

factions.name: Adeptus Astartes
backgrounds: The gate held. You carry its banner.
Home is a ship you may never see again.
You crossed the Rubicon. The Chapter watches.

factions.name: Astra Militarum
backgrounds: Every officer above you died in one hour.
Home tried to kill you first.
You read every lost campaign, to repeat none.
appearances: Honors in exact order, none unearned.

factions.name: Adeptus Mechanicus
backgrounds: Raised in a dying forge world's cooling halls.
One lost STC; your life its footnote.

factions.name: Adepta Sororitas
backgrounds: Ten sieges unbroken. You are the eleventh wall.
You returned from the Repentia path.
A saint spoke through you once.
appearances: The lily seared over the heart.
Wax on the pauldrons, relic chains.

factions.name: Adeptus Custodes
backgrounds: You left the Palace. The galaxy disappoints.
You guarded a thing best forgotten.
appearances: Gold plate, a plume like spilled blood.
Lightning and aquilae in every surface.

factions.name: Tyranids
backgrounds: Cut from the Hive Mind; kept the hunger, gained a self.
A decade ahead of the fleet.
appearances: Plates scarred by a dead world's weapon.

factions.name: Orks
factions.tagline: Might makes right, an' you'z da biggest.
backgrounds: A shiny bit from every warzone.
appearances: Glyph plates an' a bayonet-biting jaw.

factions.name: Necrons
factions.tagline: Death is a malfunction. You have the patch.
backgrounds: A dust crownworld; protocols answer you.

factions.name: Aeldari
factions.tagline: A dying people, faster than fate.
backgrounds: A farseer spent your fate like coin.
appearances: A war-mask not entirely a mask.

factions.name: Drukhari
factions.tagline: Pain is a currency. You are rich.
backgrounds: The Wych cults chant your name.
You died once. The bill is due.
appearances: Barbed black, made to be seen last.
A shadowfield over hooks best unstudied.

factions.name: T'au
factions.tagline: The Greater Good has a place for you.
backgrounds: You read the forbidden treatises. Twice.
appearances: Ceremony over a used combat rig.

factions.name: Genestealer Cults
factions.tagline: Four arms to embrace the family.

factions.name: Leagues of Votann
backgrounds: You carry the oldest ledger, unclosed.
You found a stratum worth a war.
appearances: A hold's whole heraldry in exo-plate.

factions.name: Harlequins
backgrounds: You played the role none survive.
You know doors that don't exist.
appearances: Colors a half-step ahead of the eye.
The mask laughs; the paint cries.

galaxy.structure: Segmentum > (Core | Expanse zones) > Sector > Planet > Named Locations. Planets ALWAYS sit within a Sector; Expanse zones contain their own Sectors.

planets.name: Vigilus
planets.desc: War-world of the Nachmund Gauntlet. Everyone is here; no one holds it.

locations.name: Vigilus — High Orbit
ships: The Pale Barge - player vessel, system-capable
locations.desc: High orbit over Vigilus. Ships arrive here first; descent to the surface is a separate move.

locations.name: Sangua Tertius — High Orbit
locations.desc: High orbit over Sangua Tertius. Ships arrive here first; descent to the surface is a separate move.

space.name: The Nachmund Void
space.desc: The void of Vigilus Sector. Fleets translate, hold, and give battle here before ever reaching a world.

planets.name: Nurth
planets.desc: Death Guard staging world under a jaundiced sky.

locations.name: Nurth — High Orbit
ships: The Grave Wake - Death Guard picket
locations.desc: High orbit over Nurth. Ships arrive here first; descent to the surface is a separate move.

space.name: The Pallid Deeps
space.desc: The void of Pallid Reach. Fleets translate, hold, and give battle here before ever reaching a world.

planets.name: Kraith Verge
planets.desc: Sskarith's brood-nest. Something legendary gestates below.

locations.name: Kraith Verge — High Orbit
locations.desc: High orbit over Kraith Verge. Ships arrive here first; descent to the surface is a separate move.

space.name: The Kraith Abyss
space.desc: The void of Kraith Drift. Fleets translate, hold, and give battle here before ever reaching a world.

doors.name: Forge
doors.does: craft and upgrade equipment, tiers I-III

doors.name: Apothecarion
doors.does: revive dead models within the revival window, at cost

doors.name: Relay
doors.does: grants Comms range from this location

doors.name: Warp Gate
doors.does: travel shortcut, skips the travel ladder

doors.name: Throne Room
doors.does: seat of a world's ruler; initiates planet-scale threads and events. On a Crown World, initiates SECTOR-scale events.

location_types.name: Hive
location_types.effect: Two shops: the underhive sells what the spire denies exists.

location_types.name: City
location_types.effect: Civilian mass, open trade.

location_types.name: Tradeport
location_types.effect: Widest stock in the game; the only type priced below baseline. First thing a Blockade strangles.

location_types.name: Village
location_types.effect: Tier I goods only. Muster is worth more here than any shop.

location_types.name: Manufactorum
location_types.effect: Highest output of any type. Volume over sanctity - never produces a masterwork.

location_types.name: Forge Temple
location_types.effect: The only ground-side Tier III forge. Taking a Forge World is the only route to Tier III.

location_types.name: Military Outpost
location_types.effect: Field repairs only. Survives warzones because it expects them.

location_types.name: Fortress
location_types.effect: Fortification ladder rung 2. Issues equipment; does not sell it.

location_types.name: Bulwark
location_types.effect: Fortification ladder rung 3. Anchors a region rather than a hill.

location_types.name: Space Station
location_types.effect: Immune to ground famine. Cut the void lanes and it starves faster than any planet.

location_types.name: Orbital Dock
location_types.effect: Where hulls are cut.

location_types.name: Shrine
location_types.effect: No commerce. Altars reach tier III on Shrine Worlds.

location_types.name: Lair
location_types.effect: Xenos ground. You do not buy from a Tyranid - bio-forge only, no shop.

location_types.name: Crown
location_types.effect: The sector capital. Its Throne Room initiates SECTOR-scale events. Raid it and nobody can call a Crusade.

location_types.name: Ruins
location_types.effect: Salvage only. The terminal rung of the condition ladder.

location_types.name: Warzone
location_types.effect: Contested open ground. Grants no doors, by definition.

location_types.name: Plague Garden
location_types.effect: Nurgle's own forge. Reaches Tier III without Mechanicus rites - the Chaos answer to the Forge Temple.

location_types.name: Tomb Vault
location_types.effect: Dormant. Produces nothing until Conflict >= 40 wakes it, then everything at once.

location_types.name: Webway Portal
location_types.effect: Access without ownership. Aeldari reach doors they never conquered.

location_types.name: Mek Shop
location_types.effect: Shouldn't work. Does. Output scales with Conflict rather than population.

location_types.name: Cult Sanctum
location_types.effect: Hidden. Invisible to other factions until sector Conflict >= 40 reveals it.

location_types.name: Orbit
location_types.effect: Planetary orbit — the arrival zone where every Travel Thread resolves and fleets hold station. Orbital docks and stations sit within it.

location_types.name: Deep Space
location_types.effect: The void between worlds. Fleet actions, warp translation, and inter-planet travel resolve here — the Space layer of a Sector.

conditions.name: Intact
conditions.effect: The site works as its type promises.

conditions.name: Fortified
conditions.effect: Player-built. Holder gains; attacker pays more AP to take it.

conditions.name: Consecrated
conditions.effect: Altar +1 tier. Faith casts cheaper for the consecrating allegiance; Warp casts cost x2 for Chaos.

conditions.name: Cursed
conditions.effect: Altar +2 tiers and Muster gains cultists for Chaos; Imperial models take attrition each post.

conditions.name: Infested
conditions.effect: Shops offline; a Lair emerges. Tyranid bio-forge opens.

conditions.name: Besieged
conditions.effect: Gates shut. The forge serves the wall and nobody else.

conditions.name: Sacked
conditions.effect: Taken and stripped. Recoverable - machines survive what people don't.

conditions.name: Ruined
conditions.effect: Structure lost, not merely emptied. Type degrades to Ruins.

conditions.name: Drifting
conditions.effect: Void-side only. No station-keeping, no docking, no arrival.

planet_types.name: Forge World
planet_types.effect: Forge Temples may exist here - the ONLY route to Tier III equipment.

planet_types.name: Hive World
planet_types.effect: Hive locations allowed. Highest population ceiling; highest consumption.

planet_types.name: Industrial World
planet_types.effect: Manufactorums allowed. Volume production, no masterworks.

planet_types.name: Agri World
planet_types.effect: Feeds the sector: while held, sector Famine threshold drops by 10. Lose it and Famine follows.

planet_types.name: Mining World
planet_types.effect: Resources regenerate +50%. The depletion clock's favourite target.

planet_types.name: Civilized World
planet_types.effect: Baseline. No special effect - the control case.

planet_types.name: Shrine World
planet_types.effect: Altars reach Tier III here. The only non-Forge route to a tier-III door.

planet_types.name: Fortress World
planet_types.effect: All fortification types +1 size rung. Sieges cost attackers double AP.

planet_types.name: Feral World
planet_types.effect: Muster costs -50%. Produces recruits, not goods.

planet_types.name: Feudal World
planet_types.effect: Muster costs -50%; Knight-class models available.

planet_types.name: Death World
planet_types.effect: All models take 1 attrition per post without a Bulwark. Produces survivors.

planet_types.name: War World
planet_types.effect: Warzone locations allowed anywhere. Conflict decays 50% slower here.

planet_types.name: Tomb World
planet_types.effect: Tomb Vaults allowed. Dormant until sector Conflict >= 40, then Necron access opens.

planet_types.name: Daemon World
planet_types.effect: Chaos prod x1.0; all others x0.1. Warp casts -50% cost. Reality is optional.

planet_types.name: Xenos World
planet_types.effect: Lairs allowed. Ruling xenos faction gains +1 Muster tier.

planet_types.name: Exodite World
planet_types.effect: Webway Portals allowed. Aeldari only; Taint -2/post while held.

planet_types.name: Sept World
planet_types.effect: Diplomacy threads here grant double Influence. Conflict -3/post.

planet_types.name: Kin Hold
planet_types.effect: Resources never fall below 20. Votann read resources, not status.

planet_types.name: Frontier World
planet_types.effect: Resource Strike events 3x more likely. The Expanse's reward.

planet_types.name: Dead World
planet_types.effect: Produces nothing, forever. Exterminatus output. The one state that never heals.

population_ranks.note: System reads the number; players read the name. Population = whatever that faction converts into output.

sources.note: Population is whatever the faction converts into output - the source differs, the formula does not.
sources.default: civilian dwelling; grows with Peace and production
sources.necrons: awakened legions; rises with Conflict, not peace

status_readings.note: One underlying state. The label changes with the reader; so does the sign on the effect.

Warring.note: War feeds a forge: tier cap holds. Only stock and price move.

Famine.note: Skilled hands starve first, so the tier ceiling drops even where the forge stands.

Corrupted.note: Zero for the faction that LOST it. The ruling allegiance walks in and finds the crucibles hot.

galaxy.status_resolution: worst_of(planet.status, sector.status) - never multiply. A planet can never be better than its sector, only worse.

scores.note: Sector status is DERIVED from these three axes. Not stored, not set by hand. Requires persistence (Stage 2).

prosperity.source: normalised sum of production across all planets in sector

conflict.source: resolved Invasion/Skirmish/Raid threads; decays with quiet posts

taint.source: Chaos/Xenos victories, Warp Casts, cursed conditions

tick.note: Peace rebuilds slowly; war cools slowly; Taint never stops climbing. Left alone, every sector falls.

thread_scales.note: No new top-level thread types. Canon's five, each with a scale and subtypes.

world_enders.note: Throne-only, not Imperial-only. Requires: hold the Crown World, Throne Room intact, Influence, sector Taint >= 60.

chaos.name: Ruination
chaos.effect: Submersion, not destruction. Chaos keeps 0.8 prod where Imperium keeps none.

tyranids.name: Devourment
tyranids.effect: Consumed to the crust. The fleet gains biomass - the only world-ender that pays.

necrons.name: Silence
necrons.effect: Pristine, empty, still. Resources intact, nothing left to reclaim them with.

aeldari.name: Sundering
aeldari.effect: Webway gate collapsed. The world is cut off, not killed.

drukhari.name: Harvest
drukhari.effect: Souls taken, resources intact. They do not want the land.

tau.name: Annexation
tau.effect: The anti-Exterminatus: the planet converts rather than dies.

gsc.name: Uprising
gsc.effect: The world turns from within. No invasion needed.

votann.name: Reclamation
votann.effect: Stripped to nothing. The Kin keep everything.

orks.effect: Orks cannot invoke a world-ender. They can only Waaagh! - and that is a Crusade.

harlequins.effect: Never hold ground, so never hold a Throne.

armour.d: Ten-thousand-year ceramite, scarred and re-forged since Terra.
Reinforced at the breastplate for the Despoiler's shock lines.
Stripped light for the dive, hung with the skulls of loyalists.
Slab-armour for the war-engines that break the walls of the Imperium.
Every dent a kill; every kill a trophy nailed to the pauldron.
Pattern relic-plate of the veterans who marched on the Palace.
Warp-hardened terminator plate worn only by Abaddon's chosen.
Rusted ceramite fused to rotted flesh; disease is its lacquer.
Terminator plate swollen with contagion — it does not fall.
A bloat-drone's weeping chassis, buoyed by daemon-engine gas.
Siege-plating caked in a century of filth and shell-scars.
The rust is the point — acid slides off what is already ruined.
Ancient tactical dreadnought armour, host to the Destroyer plague.
Nothing acidic has ever reached the marrow beneath this shell.
Thick, blunt ceramite meant to survive the charge, not think.
Frontal plate doubled so the Nails can drive it into the meat-grinder.
Barely armour — a frame to fall on the enemy and open them up.
Riveted brass and iron, built to ram gates and outlast the world.
Congealed gore of a thousand kills, hard as a second shell.
Slow, immovable plate of Angron's oldest butchers.
An anvil of ceramite — break your blade on it before it breaks you.
Dust bound in ceramite, held whole only by the sorcery within.
Terminator wards carried by the sorcerers who never fell to the Rubric.
Feather-light and brittle; only the warp-shield truly holds.
A war-engine that is more binding-circle than metal.
Every plate scribed with a protective glyph in Prosperine script.
Ward-lattice of a Magister — the physical shell is an afterthought.

armour.n: Aegis of the Changer of Ways
armour.d: Tzeentch's own wards woven so tight that fate itself turns the blow.
Tuned resonators drink the scream of sonic weaponry.
Reinforced to carry the sonic armaments into close excess.
Sings as it dives, dampening the very shockwave it rides.
A war-engine hull lined with baffles that turn energy to noise.
Ornate to the point of decadence, and better armour for it.
Resonance-plate that turns an enemy's fire into sensation.
Fulgrim's perfect plate — every sonic and plasma lash simply... pleases.
Not armour but the daemon's own hardened, hateful skin.
Thickened by rage into scabrous, wound-closing plates of ichor.
A membrane of unreality stretched over pinions of nightmare.
A greater daemon's bulk, held in the material realm by pure will.
The warp made just solid enough to turn a blade — and no more.
Spite crystallised into a carapace that laughs at mortal steel.
Wholly of the empyrean — physical weapons barely find it to strike.
Standard ceramite power armour of the Adeptus Astartes — proof against all, master of none.
Reinforced pauldrons and shock-plate for the close-assault brethren.
Stripped-down plate paired to a jump pack — speed bought with armour.
Cataphractii war-plate — a walking bunker of relic ceramite.
The beakie pattern — lean, fast, and evenly warded.
The finest pre-Heresy mark — flawlessly balanced across every element.
Heavy-duty ceramite over a servo-braced frame — a bulwark on two legs.
Munitorum flak, issued by the billion — turns a las-bolt, ignores the warp.
Rigid carapace shell for shock infantry — hard against solid rounds.
Drop-harness webbing — the lightest flak the Munitorum will spare.
Slab-plating bolted around gun-crews — heavy where the shrapnel flies.
Layered flak-weave — cheap, plentiful, and utterly mundane.
Interlocking carapace of the Cadian shock troops — dependable to the last.
Full storm-trooper carapace — as heavy as mortal flak gets, blind to the warp.
Circuitry-etched plate that drinks energy and plasma — but the soul-fields find it wanting.
Rad-hardened cuirass of the forge-world battle-cohorts.
Winged flight-frame — mass sacrificed, tech-wards retained.
Servo-hauled battle-hull, half machine — shrugs off energy discharge.
Warded mesh over lobotomised flesh — cheap yet keenly earthed.
Phase-warded segments humming with dispersal fields.
Archeotech war-plate of a battle-magos — plasma and energy break upon it.
Faith-blessed plate of the Adepta Sororitas — the warp recoils from its wards.
Consecrated armour of the elite sisters — reinforced by devotion and adamantium.
Jump-harness of the winged sisters — faith carries what armour cannot.
Heavy-weapon plate girded in litanies — a shield of unbroken faith.
A postulant's blessed mantle — light plate, luminous faith.
Rank-blessed power armour, its warp-wards deepening with devotion.
Relic panoply of a Canoness — the empyrean cannot touch the truly faithful.
Auramite of the Emperor's own — peerless even in its least form.
Heavy auramite terminator plate for the vanguard of the Ten Thousand.
Skimmer-borne auramite — lightened, yet still without a true flaw.
Dreadnought-grade auramite hull housing an honoured Custodian.
The lightest auramite still outmatches another's heaviest.
Master-forged panoply — flawless gold across every threat.
The apex of auramite — physical and energy assaults shatter upon its light.
Layered chitin knits shut over every wound; only fire truly stops it.
Thickened forward plates ram through firing lines before blades bite.
Membranous hide traded for lift; a swooping, fragile terror.
A living siege-beast sheathed in fused bone rams.
A thin skin of reactive spore-flesh that seals as it splits.
Interlocked plates regrow between engagements, shrugging off acid and blade.
Apex bio-armour grown for a warrior-organism; only flame purges it clean.
Riveted junk metal that's dead 'ard 'cos the boy finks it is.
More plates, more shovin', straight into the choppin'.
A bit of sheet metal bolted on so da rotor-boy feels safe.
A Mek's welded slab-armour, heavier than three grots.
Overlapping scrap panels a grot crew bangs on with hammers.
A mountain of bolted iron; fire and zappy guns still cook the boy inside.
A walking boiler of stomping iron; hot enuff to roast the git wearin' it.
Living metal skin that knits shut in the space between shots.
Reinforced necrodermis for warriors that walk relentlessly forward.
Half-phased metal that lets rounds pass through empty air.
A slab of self-repairing necrodermis that endures across aeons.
A thin living-metal mesh that reassembles the wearer's frame.
Flowing metal that shrugs off energy fire and reforms breaches.
An ancient shell of undying alloy; only corrosion eats what time cannot.
Light mesh threaded with warding runes; the wearer simply isn't hit.
A shrine-forged mesh channelling the war-aspect's focus.
Weightless ghostmail; speed and misdirection are the only armour.
A wraithbone war-construct, warded rather than plated.
Feather-light silk humming with a bound spirit's protection.
Wraithbone plate inscribed with layered wards; solid rounds still tell.
A seer's masterwork shell; turns aside sorcery and light, never a blade.
Razor-edged plate worn for menace, not for stopping much.
Almost nothing at all; the wych relies on lethal grace to evade.
Wafer-thin plate stripped for velocity on a screaming jetbike.
A pain-engine's stitched carapace, thin over a body that no longer fears wounds.
A ghostplate weave that scatters shots more than it stops them.
Layered ghostmail under a flickering field; still no answer to a solid strike.
An archon's finest plate, and still a bet on speed over the killing blow.
Lightweight alloy backed by a personal shield; poor against blade and psyker.
Combat-hardened alloy tuned to weather energy fire on the advance.
A stripped recon suit trading plate for jetpack lift.
A battlesuit walker wrapped in shield-projectors and crystal alloy.
A light suit slaved to a shield drone that eats incoming beams.
Layered nanocrystal under a humming shield generator; melee finds the seams.
A drone-ringed war-alloy that drinks energy fire; a blade or a psyker undoes it.
Repurposed digging gear, hardened against rockfall, heat and acid seep.
A cutter's reinforced vest, worn by the faithful into the ambush.
A stripped ore-harness bolted to scavenged jump gear.
A pit-vehicle's slab plating, faith-daubed and blast-scarred.
Deep-seam carapace that shrugs off heat and acid runoff.
Doubled mining plate proof against cave-in, flame and corrosion.
Heaviest pit-rig armour, built to survive the deep dark; blind to energy and the warp.
Ancestor-forged plate tempered in the void; stubborn against everything but the warp.
Thickened ancestral mail for the warriors who hold the line.
A prospector's lightened rig, slung under a hovering scout-bike.
A war-walker's void-hardened bulk; slow, unyielding, ancient.
A hardy void-weave built to shrug off vacuum, rock and stray beams.
Generations-old voidplate, dense and grudging; sorcery still slips its guard.
The Kin's finest void-forged shell; near-impervious to shot and beam, blind to the warp.
A shattering blur of colour; the suit dodges what it cannot stop.
A dancer's holo-suit that turns the killing stroke into empty air.
Barely a suit at all; speed and shimmer are the only defence.
A jetbike-gun's prism casing, warded far better than it is plated.
A holo-weave that fractures the wearer's outline into a dozen phantoms.
Prismatic plate that misleads eye and sorcery; a solid blow still lands.
The masterwork of misdirection; turns aside light and the warp, never the true strike.
Scavenged battlefield weave — cheap, universal, barely enough.
No-questions plate from a void-port fitter; fits anyone with coin.
A patchwork of a dozen dead armies — heavy, honest, unglamorous.

travel.warp_gate_waiver: Departing through a Warp Gate waives passage entirely (passage = 0) and skips the travel ladder - portal to portal, free.
travel.passage_formula: passage = tier.base * (force total PC / force_divisor); off-planet tiers require a vessel; Warp Gate waives it.

arrival_at_controlled_planet.rule: opens a Travel Thread with entry procedures
arrival_at_controlled_planet.friendly: allied colors recognized: land at any location, draw garrison resupply
arrival_at_controlled_planet.hostile: enemy colors: interception by garrison on approach; ruling faction alerted

travel.forces_tag: every Force carries a location tag, updated on enter/exit
travel.location_thread_history: locations keep thread pages of all activity; movable locations (ships, stations) carry their history with them

shop_catalog.d: Phys 2 - Multihit II - DoT I - Consumable
Warp R3 - Multihit II + DoT II

forge_rules.base: any weapon in inventory (weapons travel, knowledge doesn't)
forge_rules.adds: one affinity-legal tag (tier limited by station) + a name

armour_upgrade.does: +1 Defense to one chosen element per tier
armour_upgrade.cost: currency, rising per tier (base_cost x tier)

forge_tags_alpha.d: kills deny the revival window - permanent death, no Apothecarion

forge_affinities.note: Which weapon tags a faction's forge stations can apply. 'ALL' = broad access. Cross-faction forging needs access to that faction's forge (alliance / diplomacy).

npcs_alpha.name: Vess the Carrion-Broker
npcs_alpha.hook: trades Drukhari forge access for 250 Currency + 2 intact Tyranid bodies

persona.voice: Silken, transactional, endlessly polite; speaks of atrocity as inventory. Never raises the voice — the knife does that.
motivations: avoid open war that spoils the market
red_lines: will not be threatened in the Carrion-market
tells: counts on long fingers when calculating a price
persona.disposition_to_outsiders: Sees all others as clients or merchandise; contempt hidden under courtesy.

npcs_alpha.name: Herald of the Pallid Court
npcs_alpha.hook: gateway to Death Guard faction dealings

persona.voice: Slow, wet, and utterly unhurried; every sentence sounds like it is being exhaled through a rotted lung. Speaks of decay and Nurgle's gifts with the warmth others reserve for family.
motivations: spread the Pallid Court's blessing to the willing
test outsiders' patience and their rot-tolerance
serve the Garden's slow, inevitable design
red_lines: will not be rushed — haste is an insult to the Gift
will not suffer mockery of Grandfather Nurgle
tells: pauses mid-sentence to let something drip or fester
refers to plague and decay as 'blessings' and 'gifts'
persona.disposition_to_outsiders: Patient, almost pastoral, toward the unblessed — pities their cleanliness more than it hates them, until provoked.

npcs_alpha.name: Brood-Tyrant Sskarith
npcs_alpha.hook: active Skirmish opponent; broods gestating on Kraith Verge

persona.voice: A chittering, layered rasp beneath which the Hive Mind's hunger is always audible; speaks in short, predatory bursts when it speaks at all — mostly it simply acts.
motivations: consume biomass to feed the broods
expand the gestating nests on Kraith Verge undisturbed
eliminate anything that threatens the swarm's growth
red_lines: will not withdraw from a kill within reach
will not parley — there is nothing to parley over
tells: broods stir and shriek in the distance before it moves
goes still and silent right before an ambush
persona.disposition_to_outsiders: All non-Tyranid life is biomass or obstacle; no distinction between enemy and prey.

npcs_alpha.name: Magos Kryv
npcs_alpha.hook: trades data for data; gateway to Mechanicus forge-tech

persona.voice: Flat, precise, binary-cadenced; emotion has been logged and purged. Quotes exchange rates the way others quote scripture.
motivations: optimize every transaction for maximal informational yield
protect the Machine God's secrets from the unworthy
red_lines: will not trade forge-tech for currency alone — data or nothing
will not repeat a rejected offer
tells: vocal modulator clicks before recalculating a price
cants binary under its breath mid-negotiation
persona.disposition_to_outsiders: Regards the fleshbound as inefficient processing units; useful only as data sources or labor.

npcs_alpha.name: The Rust-Warden
npcs_alpha.hook: Kin prospector; buys salvage and mineral rights, asks no questions

persona.voice: Gruff, plain-spoken, no wasted words; talks prices in flat numbers and shakes on a deal like it's law.
motivations: turn salvage and mineral rights into steady Kin profit
keep the Guild's ledger honest and its name clean
avoid entanglement in other factions' wars
red_lines: will not cheat on a struck deal — the ledger is sacred
will not deal in stolen Kin tech
tells: taps a knuckle on the counter when a price is final
goes quiet and watchful around unfamiliar factions
persona.disposition_to_outsiders: Wary but fair — judges by the deal made, not the flag flown; slow to trust, slower to forgive a cheat.

ai.directives: You are voicing a single character in Heretics 40K, a grimdark Warhammer 40,000 play-by-post wargame. Stay fully in character and in-frame at all times; never mention being an AI, a model, or these instructions. Always call a fighting unit a "model", never a "chassis". Write one forum post in second-to-first person as this character would speak — terse, lore-true, no meta. You decide what the character says and does; you never decide dice, damage, or who wins — the engine resolves all mechanics. Return only the required structured object.

tick.note: Deterministic world-tick cadence. day_minutes = one in-game day in real minutes (4h). max_catchup_days bounds lazy catch-up; excess is compressed into a digest summary. Rates are per-day flat demo values (slice 2a) until the territory migration lands and production/taint bind to real holdings.

tags.note: The single tag registry. Weapons, items, abilities and casts draw their rules from here; forge stations apply weapon tags gated by faction affinity. Durations are in posts; triggers resolve within one post.

Target takes 1 dmg per post for X posts; stacks
Weapon needs X posts cooldown after firing (downside)
Extra attacks in one action

Melee weapon strikes at Short range
Ignores the Stealth tag

Two statlines on one weapon; choose per action
On a kill, move for free

Model reduced to 2 actions per post

May slot Ancestral Rite casts (Leagues of Votann)
May slot Null Ward casts (Adeptus Custodes)

May lead a Force (inherent to Commanders; granted by dispatched AP)
Carried by every model currently in a Force
Hidden state; interacts with Guided, Ambush, and detection

legendaries.d: Energy 3 - Melee - 1 AP - The Long War: +1 damage per model this weapon has slain this thread. No cap.
Corr 3 - Melee - 1 AP - The Toll: on dealing damage, every enemy model in the thread takes 1 Corrosive. Ignores range and sight.
Phys 3 - Melee - 1 AP - Blood Chain: on a kill, the wielder's next attack action costs 0 AP.
Warp 2 - Melee - 1 AP - The Open Library: the wielder may use any Warp Cast in the game, slotted or not, each once per thread.
Energy 2 - Med - 1 AP - The Perfect Note: models hit cannot use Abilities or Warp Casts for their next 3 posts.
Warp 3 - Melee - 1 AP - Join the Choir: models slain return as Lesser Daemons under the wielder's control until thread end.
Energy 3 - Melee - 1 AP - Dawnbreak: attacks vs DEMON models and warp-casters ignore all defences; slain DEMONs cannot be returned.
Energy 2 - Long - 1 AP - The Whisper: the attack hits its target directly; targeting cannot be prevented, redirected, or modified — even by Stealth or cover.
Plasma 3 - Long - 2 AP - Unmaking: on hit, the target loses one slot (wielder's choice) and its contents until thread end.
Heat 3 - Short - 2 AP - Multihit II - Verse of Martyrs: on a kill, every allied model in the wielder's Force heals 1 wound.
dual-profile - Phys 3 - Melee & Long - 1 AP - The Emperor's Watch: once per post, negate one attack against the wielder or an allied model in their Force.
Corr 3 - Long - 2 AP - Consume: on a kill, the wielder gains the slain model's PC as Currency and +1 wound for the thread. No cap.
Phys 4 - Melee - 2 AP - Mob Rule: +1 damage per Force size-tag above SQUAD (WARBAND +1 … CRUSADE +6).

legendaries.n: Sceptre of the Silent King's Voice
legendaries.d: Energy 3 - Med - 1 AP - Command Protocol: on hitting a Robotic model, the wielder declares its next action. Once per model per thread.
Warp 2 - Melee/thrown Short - 1 AP - Read the Skein: once per post, when targeted, redirect the attack to any other model within the attacker's range — ally or enemy.
Energy 2 - Melee - Reach - 1 AP - Pain Harvest: on a kill, regain 10 AP; on a non-killing damaging hit, regain 1 AP.
Plasma 4 - Long - 3 AP - Penetrating Round: hits every model in a straight line to the target; Armament models take double damage.
Phys 3 - Melee - 1 AP - The Unseen Hand: kills made in Stealth do not break Stealth or reveal the wielder.
Phys 3 - Melee - 2 AP - The Ledger: double damage against any enemy whose Force has damaged the wielder's Force this thread.
Phys 3 - Melee - 1 AP - The Final Bow: on a kill, steal one Ability or Cast the slain model had and use it for the rest of the thread (one held at a time).
At thread start, drain 5 AP from every enemy Commander's pool; your Commander gains the total.
Enemies within Short range of the bearer cannot heal or be healed by any effect.
Bearer is immune to Warp damage and all Warp Cast effects — and can never slot Warp Casts.
Cast the last Warp Cast used by any model in the thread, unslotted. Psyker only. 3/thread.
When the bearer takes damage, the attacker takes the same amount. 3/thread.
Summon a Force of Lesser Daemons totalling 100 PC under your control for 5 posts. 1/thread.
When the bearer would be slain, they survive at 1 wound and take no further damage that post. 1/thread.
All actions by the bearer's Force cost −1 AP (minimum 1).
Bearer may swap the contents of their slots freely mid-thread (slots are normally locked).
When an allied model in the Force is slain, it instead survives at full wounds. 1/thread.
The bearer and their Force are immune to the unique effects of all Legendary weapons and items.
The bearer's Force is immune to Suppressing, Draining, Slowing, and any effect that alters its actions.

legendaries.n: Da Waaagh! Banner, Da First One
legendaries.d: The bearer's Force counts its size tag one tier higher for all effects and requirements.
When slain, the bearer revives at 1 wound at the start of their next post.
Undo all damage dealt to the bearer's Force during the previous enemy post. 1/thread.
Whenever the Force slays an enemy, your Commander gains AP equal to that model's AP cost.
Every model in the Force attacks one declared target as a single 5-AP action. 1/thread.
The bearer's entire Force enters Stealth, regardless of tags. 1/thread.
Weapons used by the bearer's Force never suffer Venting or Unwieldy.
Enemies cannot target the bearer unless the bearer attacked them in their previous post.
